description: Primary-sourced internationalization/localization rules — externalized strings, ICU plurals, locale-aware formatting, bidi, and layout that survives translation. Re-derived from W3C i18n, Unicode CLDR/ICU, and MDN Intl.

rules.title: Externalize all user-facing strings
rules.rule: No hardcoded UI text in components; all copy lives in resource catalogs keyed for translation. Never concatenate sentence fragments — word order differs by language.
rules.do: Use a full templated message with named placeholders.

rules.title: Pluralization via ICU MessageFormat / Intl.PluralRules
rules.rule: Use ICU plural categories (zero/one/two/few/many/other) — many languages have >2 forms; never branch on n===1. Same for gendered/select messages.

rules.title: Locale-aware number/date/currency formatting
rules.rule: Format dates, numbers, and currency with Intl.* (DateTimeFormat, NumberFormat) by locale — never hand-roll. Decimal/grouping separators, date order, and currency placement vary.

rules.title: Support bidirectional (RTL) text + layout
rules.rule: Set dir on the document/region; use CSS logical properties (margin-inline-start, inset-inline) instead of left/right so layout mirrors for Arabic/Hebrew.

source.name: W3C — Structural markup and right-to-left text

rules.title: Allow for text expansion
rules.rule: Translations can be 30–200% longer than English (German/Finnish long; short source strings expand most). Avoid fixed-width text containers and truncation that hides meaning.

rules.title: Declare language with lang
rules.rule: Set the lang attribute on <html> (and on any inline foreign-language passage) so screen readers pronounce correctly and hyphenation/typography adapt.
